summary: Do not ship: a full MVP slice is absent, and Exclude filtering can disable unrelated profiles outside its declared scope. The committed tests partly encode that unsafe behavior rather than catch it.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: Cookie, Set-Cookie, CSP, and Redirect are entirely missing from the MVP
findings.body: The final schema exposes only request-header and response-header modifications. Request Cookie, Set-Cookie, directive-level CSP, and capture-group Redirect are required by the PRD, while issue 03 remains ready-for-agent with every acceptance criterion unchecked. The release evidence contains no coverage for them, so 137 passing tests and 39 smoke checks do not establish completion of the advertised MVP.
findings.recommendation: Implement issue 03 end-to-end across schema, commands, compiler, UI, import/export, golden tests, and real-browser smoke tests, or explicitly remove these stories from the release PRD before merging.

findings.title: Exclude rules escape the owning Profile's other Filter scope
findings.body: The Exclude allow rule is emitted with only regexFilter and all resource types, discarding request method, initiator, resource-type, and tab constraints. It is also emitted before the no-matching-tabs guard. Consequently, an Exclude belonging to a Profile scoped to one tab or POST requests can globally allow the URL and suppress every lower-priority Profile. The test at core/compile-tabs.test.ts:85 intentionally expects this unsafe result, despite the PRD saying different Filter kinds compose with AND semantics.
findings.recommendation: Build the allow condition through the same scoped condition path as Modification rules and skip it when tab constraints have no matches. Replace the current test with cross-profile cases proving Exclude propagation occurs only inside the owning Profile's complete Filter scope.

findings.title: A corrupted newest backup can permanently suppress self-healing
findings.body: The unchanged fast path compares only the newest manifest checksum and returns before confirming that its chunks exist and pass integrity checks. If sync loses a chunk after the manifest was committed, every later backup of unchanged profiles is skipped, leaving the newest snapshot corrupt indefinitely and preventing cleanup or replacement until profile content changes.
findings.recommendation: Only return unchanged after decodeSnapshotText or equivalent integrity validation succeeds. If the matching snapshot is corrupt, create a replacement and clean the damaged chunks; add an adapter-level regression test for a matching checksum with a missing or altered chunk.

findings.title: Required minimum Chromium version is not declared
findings.body: The PRD requires the manifest to declare the minimum version needed by the selected DNR features, but the manifest has no minimum_chrome_version. Older Chromium releases can therefore install the extension even when its response-header and DNR API semantics are unsupported, producing runtime or rule-application failures instead of an installation guard.
findings.recommendation: Derive the minimum Chromium version from every used DNR/API feature, add minimum_chrome_version to the manifest configuration, and verify the generated MV3 manifest in the build or smoke suite.

next_steps: Fix scoped Exclude compilation and replace the test that codifies global leakage.
Repair backup integrity handling and declare the supported Chromium floor.
Rerun the full unit, build, Storybook, and real-browser smoke evidence after these changes.
